private_opportunities.title: Smart Cities Awards
private_opportunities.snippet:
For more details contact Ann Whiting / awhiting@idc.com

  Nominate now
 ### The Nomination Process

  It is free to apply, and the online application takes 15–20 minutes to complete. Anyone can nominate a project, but only the public sector organization that implemented the project is eligible to win the award.

  Deadline to complete is January 15, 2026.

  We are looking for:

  + Implemented projects (not ideas or planned initiatives) that leverage emerging technologies— such as AI, GenAI, edge computing, cloud—to solve real challenges.
  + Initiatives that improve livability, resiliency, and economic opportunity for communities while fostering accessible, user friendly experiences and services. [...] Sustainable Infrastructure (e.g. Smart Buildings, Smart Water, Smart Lighting, Waste Collection, Environmental Monitoring, Resiliency)
 ### 9.

  Transportation (e.g. Connected, Autonomous, Electric and Shared Vehicles, Public Transit, Parking, Transit Hubs, Traffic Management, Transportation Infrastructure)
 ### 10.

  Urban Planning and Land Use (e.g. Permitting, Licensing, Inspection & Zoning; Digital Twins, Community Resiliency)

## Join us for the 9th year of the IDC Smart Cities and Communities Awards program!

Nominate now

## IDC Environmental Policy [...] Nominate Now

 ### Why Participate?

  The IDC Smart Cities Awards bring together people who are passionate about their work and provide an opportunity to be recognized as well as to learn and share with peers.

  Winners and finalists receive:

  + An invitation to Smart Cities Connect in Raleigh, NC, March 10-12, 2026, for an awards ceremony and IDC reception.  Winners receive an award and a speaking opportunity during the awards ceremony.
  + One hour of IDC analyst expertise to use in 2026.  Winners also receive an IDC report of their choice.
  + A social media kit and coverage in IDC blogs and publications.
  + Current IDC clients are featured in a Winner’s Spotlight with opportunities for participation in online events and webinars.

executive_takeaway: The biggest opportunity this week lies in the SmartLA 2028 initiative, which aligns well with PARTTEAM's capabilities in digital signage and self-service kiosks integrated with IoT and 5G technologies. Leadership should prioritize conducting detailed research into the specific requirements and competitive landscape of this initiative to assess potential entry strategies. Additionally, exploring partnerships for the Smart Cities Awards could enhance visibility and credibility in the sector. Monitoring the private contract opportunity for more details is also recommended to evaluate its alignment with PARTTEAM's offerings.

This aligns with our capabilities in smart city solutions, although the specific needs and competitive landscape in Los Angeles are not fully detailed.
  Action: PARTTEAM should conduct further research into the specific requirements and competitive environment of the SmartLA 2028 initiative.

However, the specific focus on Wi-Fi 7 and infrastructure upgrades may not directly align with PARTTEAM's core offerings.
  Action: PARTTEAM should explore potential partnerships or collaborations to integrate its products into smart city projects.
  Link: https://blog.smartcity.com/category/press-releases/

── EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY ───────────────────────────────
The biggest opportunity this week lies in the SmartLA 2028 initiative, which aligns well with PARTTEAM's capabilities in digital signage and self-service kiosks integrated with IoT and 5G technologies. Leadership should prioritize conducting detailed research into the specific requirements and competitive landscape of this initiative to assess potential entry strategies. Additionally, exploring partnerships for the Smart Cities Awards could enhance visibility and credibility in the sector. Monitoring the private contract opportunity for more details is also recommended to evaluate its alignment with PARTTEAM's offerings.
